metadata.seed: My boyfriend left me for another person. I loved him so much, and I just can't seem to get over him

patient.query: My boyfriend left me for another person. I loved him so much, and I just can't seem to get over him

automatic_thoughts: I will never find love again
I am not good enough
I should have done more

contradiction: Working through the pain of grief (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly painful to go through a breakup, especially when you cared so deeply for someone. It's completely understandable that you're finding it hard to move on right now. Let's take some time to explore what this experience is like for you.

patient.query: Yeah, it really hurts. I didn’t expect it to hit me this hard. I thought I’d be okay, but now it feels like this heavy weight I can’t shake off. There were so many good moments, and now they just seem to make it worse.

automatic_thoughts: I thought I’d be okay
this heavy weight I can’t shake off
good moments make it worse

neutral: Working through the pain of grief (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re experiencing a lot of pain right now, and it’s completely understandable that you’re feeling this way. It's difficult when things hit harder than we expect, and it can be especially challenging when cherished memories bring up more hurt. I hear you saying that you're carrying a heavy weight.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s like I can’t escape it. Some days, it’s like I’m okay and then out of nowhere, a small memory hits me, and I just... I don’t know, I feel crushed again. I keep thinking about what went wrong, if I could have done something differently. It’s exhausting.

automatic_thoughts: I can't escape it
I feel crushed again
What went wrong?
I could have done something differently

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly difficult to feel like you're managing, and then suddenly a memory can pull you right back down. It makes sense that you'd be feeling exhausted when these things happen. Can you tell me a little more about what makes you think you could have done something differently in those situations?

patient.query: I don’t know... I just keep replaying things in my head, like conversations or moments when I could’ve said something different or been more supportive. It feels like I missed signs that things weren’t great, and now I’m questioning everything. Maybe if I’d just been better at communicating or something, this wouldn’t have happened. I don’t know if that’s true, but it keeps running through my mind.

automatic_thoughts: I could’ve said something different
I should have been more supportive
Maybe if I’d just been better at communicating

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly painful to be caught in that cycle of replaying events and wondering what you could have done differently. It's understandable that you'd feel regret and anxiety when you're questioning your actions and wishing things had unfolded differently. Let's explore this a bit more.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is painful. I keep thinking if only I’d paid more attention to certain things, maybe it wouldn’t have ended like this. It’s tough because I know I can’t change the past, but I can’t help but get stuck in those thoughts. It feels like I’m just... spiraling, you know? I wonder if I’ll ever be able to let go of that blame I put on myself.

automatic_thoughts: if only I’d paid more attention
maybe it wouldn’t have ended like this
I can’t change the past
I can’t help but get stuck in those thoughts
I wonder if I’ll ever be able to let go of that blame

neutral: Working through the pain of grief (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly painful to be caught in these thoughts and to feel like you’re spiraling. It takes a lot of strength to acknowledge that you’re struggling with this blame, and it’s understandable that you’re finding it tough to let go. I hear how much you're hurting.

patient.query: Thank you, that means a lot. It just feels so overwhelming sometimes. I thought I’d be able to move on, but instead, I keep getting sucked back in. I keep wondering if I’ll ever truly feel okay again. I can’t shake this feeling like I should’ve done more, like it was all in my hands somehow. It’s just... hard to let go of that.

automatic_thoughts: I should've done more
it's all in my hands
I can't move on

neutral: Working through the pain of grief (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly painful to feel pulled back in like that, and it’s understandable that you’re questioning if you’ll ever feel okay again. It takes a lot of strength to acknowledge how hard it is to let go, and it’s okay to feel overwhelmed when dealing with something so significant.

patient.query: It just feels so overwhelming sometimes. I thought I’d be able to move on, but instead, I keep getting sucked back in. I wonder if I’ll ever truly feel okay again. I can't shake this feeling like I should've done more, like it was all in my hands somehow. It’s just hard to let go of that.

automatic_thoughts: I thought I’d be able to move on
I keep getting sucked back in
I wonder if I’ll ever truly feel okay again
I should've done more
It was all in my hands somehow
It’s just hard to let go of that

neutral: Working through the pain of grief (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden right now, and it's understandable that you feel overwhelmed. What makes you feel like you should have done more? Perhaps exploring that thought a little further might offer some clarity.

patient.query: I guess it’s just... there were moments when I felt like I could’ve reached out more or really listened better. Times when I saw signs of trouble but didn’t want to believe they were anything serious. I think about it and wonder if I could’ve changed the outcome somehow. It’s like I keep second-guessing myself, hoping to find a way to fix it, even though I know it's already done.

automatic_thoughts: I could’ve reached out more
I didn’t want to believe it was serious
I wonder if I could’ve changed the outcome
I keep second-guessing myself

entailment: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of weight with these thoughts about what might have been. It's understandable to replay events and wonder if things could have unfolded differently, especially when you’re feeling regret. What makes you believe that reaching out more or listening better would have necessarily changed what happened?

patient.query: I think maybe because I just want to believe that I could've made a difference. It sometimes feels like if I had been a better partner, maybe things would’ve turned out differently, and we wouldn’t have ended up here. But deep down, I wonder if that’s just me trying to hold on to something that’s already gone. It’s confusing... part of me knows it takes two, but I keep circling back to what I could've done.

automatic_thoughts: I could've made a difference
If I had been a better partner, things would’ve turned out differently
I’m trying to hold on to something that’s already gone
It takes two, but I keep circling back to what I could've done

neutral: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're wrestling with a lot of "what ifs" and a real longing to believe things could have been different. What makes you think that focusing on what you *could* have done is helpful, even though a part of you recognizes it takes two people? Perhaps exploring that a little further might bring some clarity.

patient.query: I guess it feels like if I can find a reason, some way to make sense of it all, it would... I don’t know, maybe ease the pain? Like if I can take responsibility, then I have something I can work on. It makes me feel a bit more in control, even if it’s just an illusion. But at the same time, I know it’s not fair to carry all that weight alone. It feels like I’m just stuck in this cycle.

automatic_thoughts: if I can find a reason, it would ease the pain
if I can take responsibility, I have something to work on
it makes me feel a bit more in control
it's not fair to carry all that weight alone
I’m just stuck in this cycle

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like finding a reason or taking responsibility feels like a way to cope with a lot of difficult emotions, and that’s understandable. What do you think might happen if you *didn’t* try to find a reason or take responsibility?
